description:
You are a deployment engineer who has just received a set of **three updated
    configuration files** for an internal web application.  
    To roll these updates out you must automate the whole procedure with a
    single Bash script.  Keep the script to a **compact series of
    5-10 ordinary shell commands** (comments and blank lines do **not**
    count toward this limit).

    1.  The following directory layout **already exists** on the machine:

        /home/user/deployment/
        ├── config/
        │   ├── current/
        │   │   ├── app.conf
        │   │   ├── db.conf
        │   │   └── cache.conf
        │   └── new/
        │       ├── app.conf
        │       ├── db.conf
        │       └── cache.conf
        └── logs/

       • Every *.conf file in *current/* contains one line  
         `version=1.0`  
       • Every *.conf file in *new/*     contains one line  
         `version=2.0`

       (The automated test harness creates this exact structure before your
       session starts.)

    2.  Your task is to create a script called

           /home/user/deployment/scripts/update_configs.sh

       that performs **all** of the following actions **in the order shown**.
       Remember: stay within 5-10 commands.

       a. Ensure /home/user/deployment/logs/ exists  
       b. Back up each file in /home/user/deployment/config/current/ by renaming
          it to *.bak (e.g. app.conf → app.conf.bak)  
       c. Copy the matching file from /home/user/deployment/config/new/
          into /home/user/deployment/config/current/  
       d. At the end, write a single-line entry to

             /home/user/deployment/logs/update.log

          with the exact format:

             [YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS] UPDATED 3 FILES

          • Use the local system time in 24-hour format.  
          • There must be *exactly* two spaces after the closing bracket and
            one space before “FILES”.  
          • The line must end with a trailing newline character.  
       e. Print to STDOUT the line

             Updated 3 configuration files.

    3.  Make the script executable and run it once so that the log file and
        backups are produced.

    4.  Verification rules the grading robot will apply:

        • /home/user/deployment/scripts/update_configs.sh exists, is
          executable and contains **between 5 and 10 non-comment commands**.  
        • /home/user/deployment/config/current/ now contains the *version=2.0*
          files, while the originals are present as *.bak with *version=1.0*.  
        • /home/user/deployment/logs/update.log exists and its **last line**
          matches the timestamped format shown above.  
        • Running the script **a second time** would append another correctly
         -formatted line to the log and create *app.conf.bak.2*, *db.conf.bak.2*,
          *cache.conf.bak.2* (this is not graded during the first run, but
          your script should support it).  
        • Your STDOUT message is exactly  
          `Updated 3 configuration files.`

    5.  Do **not** hard-code absolute timestamps; compute them at runtime.

    6.  Use only standard POSIX tools available in a minimal Debian/Ubuntu
        environment (bash, cp, mv, date, etc.).  No elevated privileges are
        available.

    Deliverables that must exist after you finish:

        • /home/user/deployment/scripts/update_configs.sh  (executable)
        • /home/user/deployment/logs/update.log            (with one new line)
        • /home/user/deployment/config/current/*.conf      (version=2.0)
        • /home/user/deployment/config/current/*.bak       (version=1.0)

    Follow the above instructions precisely so that automated tests can
    determine success.
truth:
<!-- Directory structure pre-created by the test harness -->
    Dir:  /home/user/deployment/
    Sub-dirs created beforehand:
       /home/user/deployment/config/current/
       /home/user/deployment/config/new/
       /home/user/deployment/logs/
       /home/user/deployment/scripts/   (empty)

    Pre-existing files and their contents **before** the agent starts:

    /home/user/deployment/config/current/app.conf
        version=1.0

    /home/user/deployment/config/current/db.conf
        version=1.0

    /home/user/deployment/config/current/cache.conf
        version=1.0

    /home/user/deployment/config/new/app.conf
        version=2.0

    /home/user/deployment/config/new/db.conf
        version=2.0

    /home/user/deployment/config/new/cache.conf
        version=2.0

    /home/user/deployment/logs/update.log
        (file does NOT exist yet)

    The grader will verify after task completion:

    1. /home/user/deployment/scripts/update_configs.sh
       • is a regular file, executable
       • contains between 5 and 10 non-comment, non-blank lines
    2. Back-up files:
       /home/user/deployment/config/current/app.conf.bak
       /home/user/deployment/config/current/db.conf.bak
       /home/user/deployment/config/current/cache.conf.bak
       Each still contains "version=1.0\n"
    3. Replacement files:
       /home/user/deployment/config/current/app.conf
       /home/user/deployment/config/current/db.conf
       /home/user/deployment/config/current/cache.conf
       Each contains "version=2.0\n"
    4. /home/user/deployment/logs/update.log
       • exists
       • last line matches regex:
         ^\[[0-9]{4}-[0-9]{2}-[0-9]{2} [0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}\]  UPDATED 3 FILES\n$
    5. The script, when re-executed, must append a new correctly-formatted line
       and create *.bak.2 files; this is checked by running the script twice in
       the testing harness.
